meta.title: Human neural-circuit model
meta.terminology_note: The state->circuit structure is the 'co-activation table' (edge-list form). 'Matrix' term retired.
meta.weights_are: qualitative expert-consensus scaffold, NOT measured effect sizes or coupling coefficients
meta.coactivation_columns_note: co-activation edges use a behaviourally-differentiating subset of circuits as endpoints; the full circuit index below is larger
key_caveats: Affiliation/oxytocin (AFF-01) is animal-dominant; human intranasal-OT effects are weak/inconsistent.
Action-observation ('mirror', SOC-02) exists but its role in action UNDERSTANDING is contested; it is built by associative learning.
Classical Broca-Wernicke language model (LANG-03) is superseded by the dual-stream model (LANG-01/02).
Working-memory maintenance mechanism (persistent vs activity-silent vs oscillatory) is unresolved.
The cortical 'pain matrix' (S1/S2/insula/ACC) is a body-salience system, not pain-specific.
Polyvagal theory's core premises are widely regarded as refuted; PAR-01 uses standard autonomic anatomy.
Gut-brain/microbiome behavioural effects (ENS-01) are largely animal-model-based; human causality uncertain.
This is a STATIC co-activation table, not a temporal/dynamical model.
RDoC has no dedicated pain construct; pain is placed across sensory/negative-valence here.
Executive function fractionates into a Common-EF factor plus updating- and shifting-specific components; 'inhibition' shows no reliable variance beyond Common EF (Miyake/Friedman).
The rostro-caudal PFC control gradient (concrete caudal -> abstract rostral) is well supported, but whether the rostral pole or mid-lateral PFC is the true apex is disputed.
Frontal white-matter tracts (TRACT-*) are structural substrate and carry no co-activation weight; they are listed in the circuit index only.

PFC-DL.name: Dorsolateral PFC (BA9/46): WM manipulation, planning, top-down control [=EXEC-01]

PFC-VM.name: Ventromedial PFC (BA10m/11m/14/25): value integration, emotion regulation, self [~REW-04/THREAT-04]

PFC-FP.name: Frontopolar/rostral PFC (BA10): prospective memory, branching/multitasking, relational integration, metacognition, internal/external attention gateway

FEF-01.name: Frontal eye fields (BA8): oculomotor attention control [=DAN-01]

TRACT-SLF.name: Superior longitudinal fasciculus (I/II/III)+arcuate: frontoparietal/frontotemporal control, attention, language

TRACT-FAT.name: Frontal aslant tract: post.IFG<->pre-SMA/SMA/ACC (L: speech initiation; R: inhibitory control)

TRACT-FS.name: Frontostriatal fibres (ant. limb internal capsule): PFC<->striatum (CSTC loops)

developmental_parameters.functional_maturation: early childhood; refines w/ vmPFC to adolescence

developmental_parameters.sensitive_period_gates: early-life sensitive period for HPA set-point

developmental_parameters.peak_or_milestone: posterior maps early; ant insula late

developmental_parameters.sensitive_period_gates: NOT innate: built by paired observe/execute experience
PHONOLOGY CP: native-speech discrimination narrows in 1st year
L1 GRAMMAR sensitive period to ~puberty (graded, not a cliff; L2 age-effect monotonic)

developmental_parameters.peak_or_milestone: autonomic reflexes functional at birth; cortical modulation matures later

developmental_parameters.sensitive_period_gates: PERINATAL steroids organise sex-typed circuits; puberty activates

developmental_parameters.peak_or_milestone: thyroid critical for early brain development
developmental_parameters.sensitive_period_gates: EARLY thyroid deficiency -> irreversible CNS effects (sensitive)
perinatal nutrition/leptin surge programs feeding circuits

developmental_parameters.peak_or_milestone: descending inhibition IMMATURE at birth, matures over childhood
developmental_parameters.sensitive_period_gates: immature descending inhibition -> heightened infant pain sensitivity

developmental_parameters.peak_or_milestone: ENS forms prenatally; microbiome colonisation postnatal
developmental_parameters.sensitive_period_gates: early microbial colonisation window may influence gut-brain signalling (animal-dominant)
no discrete CP; supports highest-order/temporally-extended control, matures last
